title: Autonomy Kernel Audit Event V1
description: One append-only log entry. The audit log is the kernel's own rank-appropriate truth source (analogous in spirit to the lane manifest's truth_check_history[], but scoped to the kernel's own actions, not a single lane's). Never edited or deleted -- corrections are new events, never mutations of history (THREAT_MODEL.md #12).

event_id.description: UUID or ULID, unique per event.

sequence.description: Monotonically increasing, gapless within one audit log file. A reader detecting a gap treats it as an integrity failure (THREAT_MODEL.md #12), not a tolerable skip.

prev_event_hash.description: Optional hash chain: sha256 of the previous event's canonical JSON serialization, or null for the first event in the log. When present, a mismatch on verification is an integrity failure and triggers the CRITICAL notification path (NOTIFICATION_TAXONOMY.md). Optional at schema level because AUT-2 may ship the append-only guarantee before the hash-chain hardening; both are acceptable schema-valid states, but a kernel claiming PROGRAM_COMPLETION_DEFINITION.md certification must have the hash chain populated.

event_type.description: Closed vocabulary. New event types require a schema version note (append-only additions are fine as a minor doc update; this list is not expected to be exhaustive forever, but is not free-text).

phase.description: Every mutating action MUST emit an 'intent' event immediately before attempting the action and an 'outcome' event immediately after, as two separate log entries with the same idempotency_key in detail. This is what makes a crash mid-action forensically visible: an intent with no matching outcome is the exact signal CRASH_RESTART_SEMANTICS.md 3 reconciles against. Non-mutating events (gate_result, kill_switch_checked, etc.) use 'info'.

actor.description: Who caused this event. 'owner' for owner-initiated mode changes and kill-switch engagement; 'kernel' for autonomous kernel actions including auto-rollback/auto-halt; 'claude'/'codex' only for events reporting an executor's own dispatch outcome.

mode.description: Kernel Mode at the moment of this event, always recorded even for owner-initiated events, so the log alone (without cross-referencing execution-state) answers 'what was the system allowed to do when this happened.'

severity.description: Maps directly to NOTIFICATION_TAXONOMY.md's severity column. Not every event at a given severity necessarily triggers a notification (the taxonomy also gates on event_type); this field records the event's own severity, the taxonomy decides notify/suppress.

idempotency_key.description: Required (non-null) whenever phase is 'intent' or 'outcome'. Matches dispatch_packet_v1.schema.json's idempotency_key for dispatch/merge events.

detail.description: Event-type-specific payload. Free-form object but must be JSON-serializable and must not contain secrets, tokens, or credential values under any key -- a CI grep guard (AUT-2/AUT-4 scope) rejects any audit event containing a recognizable secret pattern before it is written.
